theme_strategy.rationale: Designed for 24/7 IT monitoring. Uses the 'Tactical Command Center' archetype (Archetype 2 adapted for NOC). Deep obsidian bases prevent eye strain while high-visibility signal colors instantly draw attention to critical network faults.

fonts.instruction: Use for top-level headers, metric numbers, and dashboard section titles. Apply 'tracking-tight' to make it feel dense and engineered. NEVER use Inter for headings.
Clean, technical, and objective. Use for standard table rows, labels, and forms.
CRITICAL: Use for all IP addresses, Syslog raw data, SNMP payloads, timestamps, and device IDs. It reinforces the 'terminal' NOC aesthetic.

layouts.spacing_philosophy: High Density Grid (Dashboard Mode). Tight gaps (gap-4 or gap-6). Every pixel must be utilized. No jagged bottoms in grid rows.
layouts.container_style: Grid Borders ('The Technical Look'). Use border-collapse style layouts with 1px solid borders (border-zinc-800). Expose the skeleton. No heavy drop-shadows. Flat, tactical minimalism.
layouts.border_radius: Sharp edges. Use 'rounded-none' or maximum 'rounded-sm' (2px to 4px). No pill-shaped buttons in the main dashboard workspace to maintain rigid engineering alignment.

login.layout: Split screen. Left: Dark atmospheric server rack image. Right: Sharp, boxy login form with mono-spaced branding ('NOC // COMMAND').
login.interactions: Subtle hover states on the input fields (border glow). 'Enter' button should feel like a hardware terminal key.

sections: Top Row: 4 Metric widgets (col-span-1 each) showing alert counts by severity.
Middle: Alert Trends Chart (col-span-3) + Live Syslog Stream (col-span-1, scrolling vertically).

alert_list.features: Sticky header. Monospaced columns for Time, IP, Device. Left sidebar or top filter bar for Client, Severity, Device Type. 'Acknowledge' quick-action buttons on hover.

alert_detail.layout: Two-pane view. Left: Structured metadata (Client, Status, Timeline). Right: Terminal-styled block containing the full raw Syslog/SNMP trap data with syntax highlighting.

components.strategy: Use Shadcn UI extensively but strip 'Vercel-look'. Force sharp corners, flat backgrounds, and 1px borders.
components.cards: DO NOT use standard Shadcn cards with soft shadows. Build custom 'Panel' divs with sharp borders.
components.buttons: Square or slightly rounded (rounded-sm). Interactive states must increase contrast. Explicit hover text colors required.
components.charts: Use Shadcn charts (recharts). Style lines/bars with the severity color palette. Grid lines should be very subtle (#27272A).
components.icons: Use @phosphor-icons/react for dense, technical dashboard iconography. Example: 'ShieldWarning', 'HardDrives', 'Activity'.

visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Avoid in the main data views. Can be used ONLY for sticky top navigation or critical modal overlays (backdrop-blur-xl with a sharp 1px border).
visual_enhancers.motion: Minimalist. No bouncing or spring physics. Use fast, linear, or ease-out transitions (150ms) for hover states to feel responsive and instantaneous.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text meets at least 4.5:1 APCA. Tinted greys (zinc) are used over pure black. Focus states must have higher contrast (e.g., focus:ring-1 focus:ring-white).
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute (e.g., data-testid='login-submit-btn', data-testid='alert-row-critical').

image_urls.description: Dark atmospheric server rack with subtle lighting. Perfect for the NOC login screen left pane.

instructions_to_main_agent: Read this JSON fully and apply the exact fonts, colors, and layout strategies specified.
Install standard dependencies: @phosphor-icons/react, recharts, sonner, date-fns, lucide-react.
Configure Tailwind with the specified font families (Chivo, IBM Plex Sans, JetBrains Mono) and the zinc color palette.
DO NOT use Inter for any headings.
CRITICAL: The user has .js files, NOT .tsx. All React components MUST be written in standard JavaScript (.js / .jsx), DO NOT generate TypeScript.
Implement the UI using Shadcn components (adapted for JS), but strip their default rounded corners and soft shadows to match the 'Grid Borders / Technical Look'.
Create a real-time feel by simulating WebSocket data or using aggressive intervals if mocking data.
For the Syslog raw view in the Alert Details, wrap the text in <pre><code> blocks and style it like a terminal (dark bg, JetBrains Mono font, green/white text).
universal_guidelines_for_main_agent: Do not converge toward generic AI slop. Make it look like a highly engineered, professional NOC command center.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
DO NOT center align the app container (.App { text-align: center; } is banned).

Use 2-3x more spacing (padding/margins) than typical designs, but inside the 'High Density Grid', ensure components are tightly packed (gap-4) while maintaining generous internal padding (p-4 or p-6).
NEVER use emojis as icons. Always use Phosphor or Lucide icons.
Export components as named exports, and pages as default exports.
All interactive elements MUST have 'data-testid' attributes.
